description: Introduce a narrow token_info education organization lookup contract and move GetTokenInfoHandler to use that contract through the current request scope instead of resolving IAuthorizationRepository directly.
acceptance-criteria: The new lookup contract is placed in Core.External or another existing shared contract assembly that both Core and backend projects can reference without introducing circular dependencies.

A new ITokenInfoEducationOrganizationLookup contract exposes a GetEducationOrganizations method that accepts the token's EducationOrganizationId collection and returns TokenInfoEducationOrganization rows.
GetTokenInfoHandler resolves ITokenInfoEducationOrganizationLookup from requestInfo.ScopedServiceProvider or otherwise respects the scoped lifetime of backend lookup implementations.
GetTokenInfoHandler no longer calls IAuthorizationRepository.GetTokenInfoEducationOrganizations directly.
The existing IAuthorizationRepository surface and registrations remain available for legacy relationship authorization validators.
No new token_info-specific methods are added to IAuthorizationRepository.
An empty token education organization claim list returns an empty education_organizations array without invoking legacy hierarchy SQL.
The token_info response continues to include claim_set, education_organizations, namespace_prefixes, resources, services, and assigned_profiles fields.

description: Add the non-relational token_info lookup adapter that delegates to the current IAuthorizationRepository implementation.
acceptance-criteria: The default or non-relational lookup implementation delegates to IAuthorizationRepository.GetTokenInfoEducationOrganizations.
Existing non-relational PostgreSQL token_info behavior and response ordering are preserved.
The adapter handles empty education organization claims consistently with the existing repository behavior.
No relational implementation replaces IAuthorizationRepository for non-token authorization paths.

description: Update the token_info education organization response formatter to support relational discriminators and extension project endpoint names.
acceptance-criteria: Relational discriminators are parsed as ProjectName:ResourceName.
Legacy discriminators that contain only ResourceName continue to render using the standard edfi prefix.
Standard Ed-Fi rows render type values such as edfi.School and ancestor properties such as school_id.
Extension or non-Ed-Fi rows resolve ProjectName through loaded ApiSchemaDocuments and render type as projectEndpointName.ResourceName.
Ancestor id property names are derived only from ResourceName using the existing snake_case token_info contract.
The current Ed-Fi-only response shape remains unchanged for legacy discriminator values such as School and LocalEducationAgency.

description: Ensure the relational token_info lookup receives the selected relational mapping set for the current DMS instance without depending on routed resource endpoint state.
acceptance-criteria: The token_info path obtains the current MappingSet for the selected datastore instance before executing relational lookup SQL, either by adding ResolveMappingSetMiddleware to the token_info pipeline or by having the relational lookup resolve the mapping set through the existing IMappingSetProvider and runtime mapping key services.
The token_info path continues to run database fingerprint and resource-key seed validation in relational mode before using relational mapping metadata.
The mapping-context change does not require ParsePathMiddleware, ResourceInfo, or a routed resource endpoint to be present for /oauth/token_info.

Pipeline ordering tests are updated to describe the chosen mapping-set approach, including replacing the current ResolveMappingSetMiddleware omission expectation if the middleware is added to token_info.
Empty education organization claim lists return an empty education_organizations array without requiring relational mapping-set resolution or SQL execution.
Non-relational token_info behavior and pipeline ordering remain unchanged except for the new lookup abstraction.
steps-to-verify: dotnet csharpier format src/dms/core src/dms/backend src/dms/frontend

description: Build the dialect-neutral relational token_info education organization SQL model from the runtime mapping set.
acceptance-criteria: The projection enumerates concrete EducationOrganization resource root tables from the runtime relational mapping set instead of hard-coding only standard Ed-Fi table names.
Each projection arm selects DocumentId, EducationOrganizationId, NameOfInstitution, and a ProjectName:ResourceName discriminator for the concrete resource root table.
The token_info projection does not expand or alter the general EducationOrganization abstract identity view because NameOfInstitution is payload data outside that view contract.
The projection excludes descriptor resources and non-EducationOrganization resources.

The hierarchy SQL uses SourceEducationOrganizationId-to-TargetEducationOrganizationId rows for accessible education organizations and the matching source rows for ancestor fields in the returned token_info shape.

The result model returns one TokenInfoEducationOrganization row per accessible education organization and ancestor education organization pair, using EducationOrganizationId, NameOfInstitution, Discriminator, AncestorDiscriminator, and AncestorEducationOrganizationId columns.

Returned ancestor rows include valid intermediate ancestors and the concrete education organization's self row for each accessible target, not only the source education organizations directly claimed by the token.
Identifier quoting and table or column names come from the derived relational model and dialect rules.
The generated projection order is deterministic for stable SQL and test expectations.
Direct claimed education organization self rows are deduplicated against existing hierarchy self rows before projection results are returned.
The relational lookup returns a stable result set that allows GetTokenInfoHandler to preserve deterministic education_organizations array ordering by education_organization_id.
The SQL model exposes the parameters and result columns needed by both PostgreSQL and SQL Server executors.

description: Implement the PostgreSQL relational ITokenInfoEducationOrganizationLookup query.
acceptance-criteria: When relational backend is enabled with PostgreSQL, the lookup reads auth.EducationOrganizationIdToEducationOrganizationId plus the concrete EdOrg projection.

The query adds direct self rows for claimed education organization IDs that resolve to concrete EdOrg rows, even when the auth table lacks a self tuple, without duplicating an existing hierarchy self tuple.
The query never references dms.EducationOrganizationHierarchy or other legacy document-store-only hierarchy objects.
Unresolved claimed EdOrg IDs and stale auth tuples with missing source or target projection rows are ignored without failing token_info.
Empty or fully unresolved claim lists return no education organization rows without a server error.

Returned ancestor rows include valid intermediate ancestors and the concrete education organization's self row for each accessible target, not only the source education organizations directly claimed by the token.
Token education organization IDs are passed as parameters and are not inlined into SQL.
Returned rows contain the accessible education organization and ancestor education organization data needed by the existing token_info response grouping logic.
Returned rows are ordered deterministically so repeated runs produce the same token_info education_organizations array.

description: Implement the SQL Server relational ITokenInfoEducationOrganizationLookup query.
acceptance-criteria: When relational backend is enabled with SQL Server, the lookup reads auth.EducationOrganizationIdToEducationOrganizationId plus the concrete EdOrg projection.
The SQL Server query implements the same direct claim, direct self-row deduplication, unresolved claim, stale tuple, and empty claim semantics as PostgreSQL.
The query does not depend on legacy document-store-only hierarchy objects.

Returned ancestor rows include valid intermediate ancestors and the concrete education organization's self row for each accessible target, not only the source education organizations directly claimed by the token.
Token education organization IDs use the existing SQL Server relational parameter binding infrastructure and are not inlined into SQL.
The SQL Server command uses the existing mssql identifier quoting, schema qualification, and relational command execution conventions.
Returned rows are ordered deterministically so repeated runs produce the same token_info education_organizations array.
SQL Server implementation tests cover both generated SQL shape and parameter binding without requiring a DMS E2E scenario.

description: Wire token_info education organization lookup implementations into the existing backend selection path.
acceptance-criteria: Non-relational/default startup registers the legacy adapter implementation of ITokenInfoEducationOrganizationLookup.

PostgreSQL relational startup in Infrastructure/WebApplicationBuilderExtensions.cs replaces the lookup with the PostgreSQL relational implementation only when AppSettings:UseRelationalBackend is true and AppSettings:Datastore is postgresql.

SQL Server relational startup in Infrastructure/WebApplicationBuilderExtensions.cs replaces the lookup with the SQL Server relational implementation only when AppSettings:UseRelationalBackend is true and AppSettings:Datastore is mssql.
The existing IAuthorizationRepository registration is not removed or replaced by the relational token_info work in datastore modes where it is already registered.

Relational PostgreSQL service registration tests prove IAuthorizationRepository remains available for legacy authorization validators while ITokenInfoEducationOrganizationLookup resolves to the relational token_info implementation.
Service registration tests prove the selected lookup implementation for non-relational PostgreSQL, relational PostgreSQL, and relational SQL Server.

description: Add focused Core unit coverage for the token_info handler, formatter, legacy adapter, and mapping-context boundary.
acceptance-criteria: Unit tests prove GetTokenInfoHandler resolves and uses ITokenInfoEducationOrganizationLookup instead of IAuthorizationRepository.
Unit tests cover empty education organization claim lists returning an empty education_organizations array without invoking backend SQL.
Unit tests prove the legacy adapter delegates to IAuthorizationRepository.GetTokenInfoEducationOrganizations for the non-relational path.
Unit tests cover Ed-Fi and extension discriminator rendering, including projectEndpointName resolution and snake_case ancestor property names.

Unit tests cover that GetTokenInfoHandler preserves the token_info response contract while using ITokenInfoEducationOrganizationLookup, including claim_set, education_organizations, namespace_prefixes, resources, services, and assigned_profiles.
Unit tests cover the chosen mapping-context setup or lookup-side mapping-set resolution so the relational lookup is not invoked with missing mapping metadata.

description: Add provider-backed PostgreSQL integration tests for the relational token_info education organization lookup.
acceptance-criteria: PostgreSQL backend integration coverage seeds generated relational EdOrg tables and auth.EducationOrganizationIdToEducationOrganizationId, invokes the lookup, and verifies the expected hierarchy-shaped projection.
PostgreSQL integration tests prove the direct claim self-row rule, direct self-row deduplication, intermediate ancestor rows, empty claims, unresolved claims, and stale tuple handling.
PostgreSQL coverage proves the lookup no longer fails with 42P01 for missing dms.EducationOrganizationHierarchy.
The tests verify deterministic returned education organization ordering for the token_info response.
Provider-backed tests execute the lookup through ITokenInfoEducationOrganizationLookup or its concrete PostgreSQL provider service rather than only comparing SQL text.
The tests use the PostgreSQL backend generated DDL integration harness rather than the Docker E2E stack.

description: Add provider-backed SQL Server integration tests for the relational token_info education organization lookup.
acceptance-criteria: SQL Server backend integration coverage seeds generated relational EdOrg tables and auth.EducationOrganizationIdToEducationOrganizationId, invokes the lookup, and verifies the expected hierarchy-shaped projection.
SQL Server integration tests prove the direct claim self-row rule, direct self-row deduplication, intermediate ancestor rows, empty claims, unresolved claims, and stale tuple handling.
The tests verify deterministic returned education organization ordering for the token_info response.
Provider-backed tests execute the lookup through ITokenInfoEducationOrganizationLookup or its concrete SQL Server provider service rather than only comparing SQL text.
The tests use the SQL Server backend generated DDL integration harness rather than the Docker E2E stack.
No SQL Server DMS E2E scenario is added for this story.
steps-to-verify: Start a local SQL Server test container if ConnectionStrings__MssqlAdmin is not already configured, for example: docker run --rm --name dms-codex-mssql -e ACCEPT_EULA=Y -e MSSQL_SA_PASSWORD='<StrongPassword>' -p 1434:1433 -d mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2022-latest

description: Use the existing token introspection happy-path scenario as the relational PostgreSQL E2E signal.
acceptance-criteria: Features/Security/TokenIntrospection.feature scenario 01 is tagged with @relational-backend and the appropriate relational shard tag.
No duplicate relational-only happy-path token_info scenario is added for the same application/token wiring.
The relational E2E run provisions the relational database through build-dms.ps1 E2ETest and uses the relational environment file.
The scenario returns 200 for POST /oauth/token_info with a valid token and verifies the expected education_organizations hierarchy response.
The E2E signal demonstrates the endpoint no longer returns PostgreSQL 42P01 for dms.EducationOrganizationHierarchy.
The generated TokenIntrospection.feature.cs file is updated if the repository requires checked-in generated Reqnroll bindings after retagging the scenario.
The E2E feature tag guardrail tests continue to pass after scenario 01 is retagged.
